candidate_llm_application_summary: Layer-wise monotone-decreasing input perturbation (token swap + embedding noise) schedule with depth during pretraining, forcing early layers to learn exposure-tolerant robust representations (stress-gradient stratification).

results.title: Intertidal zone — Wikipedia
results.snippet: Source-domain: vertical zonation by tidal-exposure tolerance.
results.rationale: Source-domain biological description only; no LLM training mechanism present.

results.title: Rocky shore habitat — Coastal Wiki
results.rationale: Source-domain ecology only; not LLM relevant.

results.title: Effects of Intertidal Position on Barnacle Metabolism — PMC
results.snippet: Source-domain: zonal stress impact on barnacle physiology.
results.rationale: Biological physiology paper; no ML/LLM training analogue.

results.title: Layer-wise Gaussian Noise Injection (2025/9)
results.snippet: LLM-side: layer-wise Gaussian noise allocation for privacy + objective.
results.rationale: Explicitly allocates Gaussian noise across layers with a per-layer schedule. Functional core (depth-conditioned noise allocation across layers during training) matches the candidate's monotone layer-noise schedule despite different motivation (privacy vs robustness/specialization). Crosses func>=0.7 and sem>=0.7 and kw>=2.

results.title: LLM Reasoning Distracted by Irrelevant Context (2025/5 v2)
results.snippet: LLM-side: continued pretraining with irrelevant-context injections improves robustness.
results.rationale: Layer-uniform input augmentation for robustness; lacks the depth-conditioned monotone schedule that is the candidate's defining feature.

results.title: Noise Perturbation Fine-tuning (NPFT) (2024/12)
results.snippet: LLM-side: outlier-weight noise perturbation during PEFT.
results.rationale: Weight noise during PEFT for quantization; not input-perturbation schedule across depth during pretraining.

results.title: Crown Frame Reverse: Layer-Wise Scaling (2025/9)
results.snippet: LLM-side: layer-wise scaling variants for pretraining.
results.rationale: Acknowledged in candidate's novelty claim as adjacent prior art; modulates capacity/scaling across layers with monotone schedules but does NOT inject input-perturbation noise as the depth-conditioned variable. Functional axis (capacity vs input-noise) is different — does not cross 0.7.

results.title: Layer-wise Sparsity for LLMs (2025/2)
results.snippet: LLM-side: monotonically-increasing layer-wise sparsity for reconstruction stability.
results.rationale: Monotone schedule discipline but on sparsity, not input perturbation. Candidate's novelty claim already names this as prior art being differentiated; structurally adjacent but different functional channel.

verdict_reasoning: Rank 4 (Layer-wise Gaussian Noise Injection, 2509.04232) crosses all three OR-rule arms (func 0.76, sem 0.72, kw 2). One hit suffices for FAIL under the FROZEN OR rule. The candidate's depth-conditioned layer-wise noise schedule is operationally anticipated by this paper despite differing motivation.
agreement_with_judge_pass1: Partial. Pass-1 marked rank 4 and rank 7 both as functional hits. Verifier confirms rank 4 hit but disagrees with rank 7: Crown-Frame-Reverse modulates capacity/scaling per layer, not input-noise per layer — different functional channel, so does not reach 0.7. Final verdict (FAIL) is consistent regardless because rank 4 alone triggers FAIL.
